metadata.changes_from_iteration_1: Skill: web search fallback, vault citation, Freedom Ladder status bar, 3000-word limit, stronger do-nothing framing. Evals: dropped web search assertion, added vault-integration/framework/conservative-bias assertions.

comparison_to_iteration_1.note: With-skill stable at ~97%. Baseline dropped from 96% to 59% because new assertions test for skill-specific capabilities (vault integration, Freedom Ladder, conservative bias) that the baseline cannot provide.

notes: The new assertions successfully differentiate skill from baseline. With-skill pass rate: 97.0%. Without-skill: 59.1%. Delta: +37.9 percentage points.

The only with-skill failure was Eval 3 (pension) vault data assertion — obsidian-cli returned FILE_NOT_FOUND and the agent couldn't fall back to direct filesystem reads. This is an execution environment issue, not a skill quality issue.
Baseline consistently fails on: Freedom Ladder level (0/3), vault data integration (0/3), output format compliance (0/1), conservative bias (0/1), and the 30% concentration threshold (0/1).

Baseline still passes domain knowledge assertions well (lending rules, ASK tax, pension pillars, IPS max) — confirming Claude has strong Norwegian finance knowledge natively. The skill's value is framework, personalization, and consistency.
Cost tradeoff: with-skill uses ~2x tokens and ~3x time. This is acceptable for major financial decisions where the quality difference is substantial.
Iteration 1 → 2 improvement: the web search fallback and vault citation instructions produced cleaner output with explicit source attribution.
